mechanism_narrative: The prevailing level of the federal funds rate exerts a direct negative effect on S&P 500 forward returns by increasing the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows; higher observed FEDFUNDS levels predict negative 20-day returns.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between FEDFUNDS level and 20-day S&P 500 forward returns is positive and statistically significant (p < 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in real retail sales signals weakening consumer demand that compresses expected corporate revenues and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when RSAFS declines month-over-month over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of low unemployment and a high federal funds rate predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days because restrictive monetary policy into a tight labor market elevates the probability of a sharp economic correction.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with restrictive monetary policy
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE < 5% and FEDFUNDS > 5% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, an increase in consumer price inflation forces equity markets to reprice the path of future discount rates higher, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During identified tightening cycles, months with positive month-over-month CPIAUCSL change do not produce negative average 20-day S&P 500 returns (t-test p < 0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in housing starts signal a deepening contraction in residential investment that transmits to broader economic activity and equity earnings, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances following two consecutive monthly declines in HOUST over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output that supports corporate revenue expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with positive month-over-month INDPRO growth do not produce positive average 20-day S&P 500 returns (t-test p < 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payroll employment indicates deteriorating labor demand that precedes weaker consumer spending and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when PAYEMS declines month-over-month over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month decline in retail sales and rise in unemployment create a joint demand-income contraction that amplifies downside earnings revisions and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines month-over-month and UNRATE rises month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when both RSAFS declines and UNRATE rises month-over-month over any 3-year rolling window.
